teamObjective.explanation: With only low non-trump clubs, player 0 is unlikely to retain control. The best team goal is to lead the weakest possible card so partner (player 2) has the lowest bar to take over the lead, while denying opponents a chance to burn a high card cheaply.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds 3 unknown cards. Because player 0 already holds four low clubs, partner is slightly more likely than random to hold at least one mid-to-high card that can beat a low single club, but this remains a weak belief with no public signals.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards (tagged near finish), suggesting they may be holding one or more strong cards they intend to use to go out soon. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards. Both opponents will likely try to beat any single lead; player 1 in particular may use this trick to advance their finish plan.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of clubs is the most conservative opening play. It gives the least value to whichever opponent wins the trick, preserves the slightly higher 4-5-6 for later, and creates the lowest possible threshold for partner to take over the lead.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the highest low card spends our best remaining card without a clear upside; if the trick is won cheaply by opponents, we lose our strongest card and still cede control.
The 5 is still higher than necessary for an opening lead from a purely weak hand; it provides no better signaling value than the 3 and leaves a wider gap to partner's possible cards.
Although close to the 3, the 4 is strictly higher and offers no compensating advantage in a situation where all our cards are low and we want to minimize the trick's value.

risks: Opponent 1 may beat the low lead and use the gained initiative to finish their hand
Partner may be unable to take the trick, leaving opponents in control
A weak opening lead may simply lose the lead without gaining useful information
riskAssessment.mitigation: By choosing the absolute lowest card, we force opponents to spend at least as much rank as necessary to win and keep our remaining cards as high as possible, giving us the best chance to regain or support the lead later.

notes: No hidden cards are asserted as facts; all beliefs are probabilistic and grounded only in the public new_game event and hand counts.
